title: illecebrum glomeratum
intro: Illecebrum glomeratum, also known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky mouse ear chickweed, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Illecebrum glomeratum has been used to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, providing relief from symptoms like itching, redness, and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and it is also used in some traditional remedies for its expectorant properties to help relieve coughs and congestion.
